repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body: ## Problem The snippet preview is not being highlighted by treesitter for completion items from servers which don't support `completionItem/resolve` (like gopls). This was broken by #38428. ## Solution Call `update_popup_window` after updating the completion item with the snippet preview. I've added assertions to the `selecting an item triggers completionItem/resolve + (snippet) preview` test case which covers the snippet preview being shown since no tests failed when I removed the `nvim__complete_set` call which actually populates the preview on this codepath. ### Before ### After

comment_data.body:
Ah nice, I didn't know about `vim.treesitter.highlighter.active`. I was wondering how to explicitly check that treesitter is being used. I've added an assertion on it to the assertions on the resolved item snippet preview as well.

I've removed the test case I added and merged its assertions into `selecting an item triggers completionItem/resolve + (snippet) preview`. I've kept around the `screen:expect` assertions though otherwise nothing was verifying that the contents of the preview are correct on this codepath (replacing `{ info = ('```%s\n%s\n```'):format(vim.bo.filetype, text) }` with `{ info = 'foo' }` caused no tests to fail).

There is no need to add a screen output for this case. The preceding screen output has already demonstrated that the preview buffer and Treesitter are functioning correctly. Here, it suffices to simply assert that Treesitter has been enabled for this specific item's preview buffer.

I figured it made sense to explicitly check that treesitter is being used here as well since we want the same behaviour here as in the case i've added below. Or are we happy with this being implicitly verified by the screen output showing the preview highlighting with the `100` attribute ID?

>The preceding screen output has already demonstrated that the preview buffer and Treesitter are functioning correctly.

Yes, when the completion items are populated from `completionItem/resolve` but not when the server does not support this.

I'm happy to just use the assertions which you've suggested but they will still pass if I make this change
```patch
diff --git i/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/completion.lua w/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/completion.lua
index 74873d96f2..636c737798 100644
--- i/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/completion.lua
+++ w/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/completion.lua
@@ -805,7 +805,7 @@ local function on_completechanged(group, bufnr)
           local text = parse_snippet(lsp_item.insertText or lsp_item.textEdit.newText)
           local windata = api.nvim__complete_set(
             data.selected,
-            { info = ('```%s\n%s\n```'):format(vim.bo.filetype, text) }
+            { info = 'foo' }
           )
           update_popup_window(windata.winid, windata.bufnr, protocol.MarkupKind.Markdown)
         end
```
whereas my assertions would fail.
I'm just trying to add a small bit of (what seems like) missing test coverage 🙏
yes `100` highlight attribute and the `conceal=2` is also working. This serves as ample proof that `update_popup_window` has successfully configured the Markdown preview.
Hardcoding 'foo' bypasses the snippet parsing process... Could any server response result in this outcome.. if have I think it's fine to add a screen output.

could be simplified.
```suggestion
    wait_for_pum()
    eq(true, n.fn.complete_info({ 'selected' }).preview_bufnr == nil)
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1474,6 +1474,46 @@ describe('vim.lsp.completion: integration', function()
       {1:~                                                 }|*15
       {5:-- INSERT --}                                      |
     ]])
+
+    n.command('lua vim.lsp.buf_detach_client(0, ' .. dummy_client_id .. ')')
+    -- Server which doesn't support completionItem/resolve
+    create_server('dummy2', {
+      isIncomplete = false,
+      items = {
+        {
+          insertText = 'package main',
+          insertTextFormat = 1,
+          kind = 9,
+          label = 'package main',
+          sortText = '0001',
+        },
+        {
+          insertText = 'package ${1:name}',
+          insertTextFormat = 2,
+          kind = 9,
+          label = 'package',
+          sortText = '0002',
+        },
+      },
+    })
+    feed('<ESC>S<C-x><C-O>')
+    -- No popup shown for item without snippet
+    screen:expect([[
+      package main^                                      |
+      {12:package main Module }{1:                              }|
+      {4:package      Module }{1:                              }|
+      {1:~                                                 }|*16
+      {5:-- INSERT --}                                      |
+    ]])

comment_data.body:
>Could any server response result in this outcome..

I don't really see what this has to do with it. 

I'm just trying to verify that the parsed snippet gets correctly shown in the preview window. My hardcoding in the example above just shows that your suggested assertion would still pass even when the shown snippet is clearly wrong (hardcoded). A more likely kind of bug that would be caught by my assertions is something more subtle like some extra whitespace:
```patch
diff --git i/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/completion.lua w/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/completion.lua
index 74873d96f2..cb28543a98 100644
--- i/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/completion.lua
+++ w/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/completion.lua
@@ -805,7 +805,7 @@ local function on_completechanged(group, bufnr)
           local text = parse_snippet(lsp_item.insertText or lsp_item.textEdit.newText)
           local windata = api.nvim__complete_set(
             data.selected,
-            { info = ('```%s\n%s\n```'):format(vim.bo.filetype, text) }
+            { info = ('```%s\n %s\n```'):format(vim.bo.filetype, text) }
           )
           update_popup_window(windata.winid, windata.bufnr, protocol.MarkupKind.Markdown)
         end
```

comment_data.body:
My overarching point is still that 

>The preceding screen output has already demonstrated that the preview buffer and Treesitter are functioning correctly.

doesn't hold because those checks don't defend against the popup preview being incorrectly populated on the codepath where the server can't `completeionItem/resolve`.

comment_regular_data.body: The [ubuntu asan clang functionaltest](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/actions/runs/23690464038/job/69017063479?pr=38534#logs) failure in `test/functional/terminal/tui_spec.lua` looks unrelated. I can't actually tell what failed in [FreeBSD](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38534/checks?check_run_id=69017064267) but `functionaltest` passed in all other jobs and locally so assume both failures are flakes.
Thanks for pushing completion forwards on the vim and neovim sides so much 🙏
Note: this commit is currently included in the release-0.12 branch.

title: fix(lsp): highlight snippet preview when server can't completionItem/resolve
